alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the physical context (inertial-fusion-relevant configuration), narrowing the claim's scope while preserving the substance that a novel effect is exploited.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A kinetic description is useful for studying nonlinear effects in the higher dimensional momentum-position phase-space of plasma dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this conceptual conclusion about kinetic descriptions and nonlinear effects; it is not represented among the reported results.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient-based optimization of neural networks provides forcing function parameters to the differentiable solver based on initial conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a technical result about using gradient-based optimization to supply forcing parameters that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A multilevel Monte Carlo gradient estimator is unbiased and has finite variance and finite expected computational cost under certain conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A multilevel Monte Carlo gradient estimator is unbiased and has finite variance and finite expected computational cost under certain conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the theoretical result verbatim, preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The standard theory from stochastic optimization for a parametric (non-nested) expectation directly applies to the multilevel Monte Carlo gradient estimator.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The standard theory from stochastic optimization for a parametric (non-nested) expectation directly applies to the multilevel Monte Carlo gradient estimator.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Another unbiased gradient estimator with finite variance and cost can be constructed for a special case.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Another unbiased gradient estimator with finite variance and cost can be constructed for a special case.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same additional estimator and frames it as a contribution/result consistent with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interpretable machine learning techniques applied to medical datasets facilitate early and fast diagnoses and provide deeper insights into the data.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert the specific clinical outcomes (earlier/faster diagnoses or deeper insights) present in the preprint; that conclusion was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prototype-based (PB) interpretable models presented in this paper show comparable or superior performance to alternative techniques applicable in situations with heterogeneous measurements, imbalanced classes, and missing data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed models is similar to or superior compared to alternative techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the performance comparison but reports it with reduced certainty (medium vs high), so the claim's content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed strategy of averaging model parameter manifolds allows for harnessing the power of ensembles while maintaining the intrinsic interpretability of the PB models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The strategy of averaging over model parameter manifolds allows for the creation of ensembles while maintaining the interpretability of the prototype-based models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the proposed ensemble strategy; its presentation is stronger (higher certainty) in the publication than in the preprint, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models and strategies introduced in this paper address the challenges of real-world medical data while remaining computationally inexpensive and transparent.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed prototype-based interpretable models can effectively handle common issues in medical datasets such as heterogeneous measurements, imbalanced classes, and missing data.
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the preprint's combined claim into a focused claim about handling common dataset issues (Q0) and another about computational cost (Q2); this alignment captures the handling/challenge aspect while preserving overall intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed variational approach effectively solves partial differential equations governing the evolution of high dimensional probability distributions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed variational approach effectively solves PDEs governing the evolution of high dimensional probability distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The two claims are effectively identical in meaning and scope, only differing in minor wording (PDEs vs partial differential equations).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach works on the unbounded continuous domain and encodes the full probability density function through its variational parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach encodes the full probability density function through its variational parameters, which are dynamically adapted during the evolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Encoding of the full density is preserved but the published wording drops the explicit mention of the unbounded continuous domain and folds in dynamic adaptation; thus the claim is presented more tightly/clarified and somewhat narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variational parameters are adapted dynamically during the evolution to optimally reflect the dynamics of the density. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach encodes the full probability density function through its variational parameters, which are dynamically adapted during the evolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert dynamic adaptation of variational parameters; the publication repeats this point without changing its meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The benchmark cases show excellent agreement with numerical solutions and analytical solutions in regimes inaccessible to traditional computational approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method shows excellent agreement with numerical and analytical solutions for challenging tasks compared to traditional computational approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claim strong empirical agreement, but the publication softens the statement (from 'regimes inaccessible' to 'challenging tasks'), narrowing the scope and reducing certainty relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GMM-CNN features encoded with two mixture components provided a significantly better performance than standard CNN classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GMM-CNN features encoded with two mixture components provided a significantly better performance than standard CNN classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieved an accuracy in the range of 96.00–96.70% and an area under the ROC curve in the range of 99.29–99.45%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method achieved an accuracy in the range of 96.00%-96.70% and an area under the ROC curve in the range of 99.29%-99.45%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numerical performance ranges are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining GMM-CNN features from both computed tomography and X-ray images yielded the best performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining GMM-CNN features from both CT and X-ray images yielded the best performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim is the same; the publication states it as a result with higher asserted certainty but content matches directly.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed GMM-CNN features could improve the prediction of COVID-19 in chest CT and X-ray scans. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion phrasing and level of certainty are preserved between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Rehearsal-based Continual Learning approaches are a reliable solution to overcome catastrophic forgetting in Artificial Neural Networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Sudden input disruptions and memory constraints alter the consistency of predictions in rehearsal-based Continual Learning. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Continual Spectral Regularizer for Incremental Learning (CaSpeR-IL) improves the performance of state-of-the-art methods on standard benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Replayed data points of different classes increasingly mix up, interfering with classification. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method employs visual information as assistant signals to enhance general NLP tasks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The error models used in the framework are based on corruption patterns of the output of CNN operators induced by faults. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed network achieves one of the most accurate results in the abdominal CT data set with a Dice score of 55% and an average surface distance of 7.8 mm for 9 organs of various sizes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Adding 3 recursive networks is sufficient to achieve state-of-the-art results without a significant increase in inference time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method utilizes a recurrent neural network that enforces sparsity with respect to the weights of the network to suppress noise in image patches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The UNet-based model with EfficientNet as an encoder outperforms all other encoders for multiclass segmentation of sub-brain regions in 2D images.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make any claim about outperforming other encoders or broad multiclass encoder comparisons; that specific performance comparison appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model effectively detects two sub-regions, compacta (SNCD) and reticulata (SNr), in all analyzed images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The UNet-based architecture successfully segments the dorsal tier of substantia nigra pars compacta (SNCD) and reticulata (SNr) with high precision.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms successful segmentation of the same two sub-regions and emphasizes high precision, which clarifies the original detection claim without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Despite limited training data, the best model achieves a mean intersection over union (IOU) of 79% and a mean dice coefficient of 87%.
alignments.rationale: The publication's summary does not report these specific quantitative performance metrics, so this numeric result is omitted from the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data augmentation was employed to increase the sample set, providing data diversity and robust learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not mention data augmentation as a method or contribution, indicating this preprint methodological detail was not carried forward into the highlighted publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for constructing the absolute permeability map is consistent with well logging and well test measurements in oil reservoirs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research advances existing geostatistical interpolation techniques by fusing well logging and well test data to build the reservoir permeability map.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's consistency-with-measurements claim as a broader methodological advance that fuses well logging and well test data; the emphasis and certainty are somewhat moderated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The constructed permeability map is hydrodynamically similar to the original permeability map.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for estimating the spatial distribution of absolute permeability in oil reservoirs demonstrates hydrodynamic similarity to the original permeability map.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same hydrodynamic-similarity claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical simulations of production using the constructed permeability map yield quantitatively similar results to those using the original permeability map in terms of pore pressure and fluid saturations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical reservoir simulations yield comparable pore pressure and water saturation distributions between the constructed and original permeability maps.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result about comparable pore pressure and saturation/pore distribution outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a good match between the results of numerical simulations in terms of flow rates and total volumes of produced oil, water, and injected water.
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not report the specific matching of flow rates/total produced and injected volumes found in the preprint, so this claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed approach outperforms kriging in terms of numerical reservoir modeling outcomes.
alignments.rationale: This performance-vs-kriging conclusion is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel incorporation of well test interpretation results into the permeability map represents a significant improvement over existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly emphasizes novelty and improvement from incorporating well-test interpretation, which was not asserted as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed broad learning system with Takagi-Sugeno fuzzy subsystem significantly reduces the training time to about 128 seconds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel broad learning system with Takagi–Sugeno fuzzy subsystem significantly reduces training time for tobacco origin identification.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the significant training-time reduction claim but omits the specific 'about 128 seconds' figure, so the claim is clarified with less numeric detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TS fuzzy subsystem effectively improves the recognition performance of tobacco origin identification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TS fuzzy subsystem effectively improves recognition performance by extracting features from near infrared data.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the mechanism (feature extraction from NIR data) explicit while preserving the improvement-in-recognition claim, so the claim is clarified with added mechanism detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves the highest prediction accuracy of 95.59% compared to traditional classification algorithms, artificial neural networks, and deep convolutional neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves the highest prediction accuracy of 95.59% compared to traditional classification algorithms, artificial neural networks, and deep convolutional neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact performance claim and comparison set, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Incremental learning in the proposed method allows for obtaining the weight matrix of the network with minimal computation.
alignments.rationale: This operational detail about incremental learning and obtaining the weight matrix does not appear in the preprint claims and is therefore an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adapted node-place model demonstrates a relationship between station characteristics and local COVID-19 footprints.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adapted node–place model can be used to predict and monitor hotspots for COVID-19 and other pandemics’ transmission.
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from demonstrating a relationship to asserting a predictive/monitoring use (including other pandemics), so the claim is revised and broadened in application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stations with high node, place, and human mobility indices normally have more COVID-19 footprints in proximity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stations with high node, place, and human mobility indices normally have more COVID-19 footprints in proximity.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical finding with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many of the place, node, and human mobility indicators significantly impact the concentration of COVID-19 footprints.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many of the place, node, and human mobility indicators significantly impact the concentration of COVID-19 footprints.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim about significant indicators affecting footprint concentration unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The unique metric of COVID-19 footprints, derived from detailed visit history of the infected, is proposed and exploited in this study.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention this specific 'unique metric' claim from the preprint, so that preprint claim appears to have been removed from the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The physics-informed neural network (PINN) approach effectively solves the Blasius function without converting the non-linear differential equation to an initial value problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The physics-informed neural network (PINN) approach effectively solves the Blasius function without converting the non-linear differential equation to an initial value problem.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological claim with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PINN method addresses the convergence issue found in conventional series solutions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PINN method addresses the convergence issue present in conventional series solutions.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same claim about addressing convergence issues as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results produced by the PINN method are comparable to those obtained from numerical and conventional methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results produced by the PINN method are comparable to those obtained from numerical and conventional methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result-comparability claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The solution using PINNs can be extended to the negative axis, capturing the singularity of the function at η=-5.69.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The solution using PINNs can be extended to the negative axis, capturing the singularity of the function at −5.69.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the extension-to-negative-axis and singularity-capture claim exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system can effectively enhance the performance of the downstream tasks while retaining explainability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system can effectively enhance the performance of downstream tasks while retaining explainability.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same experimental-result claim with effectively identical wording and emphasis on explainability and performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlipayKG is an offline concept knowledge graph that models the historical behaviors of users and the rich content interacted by users.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlipayKG is an offline concept knowledge graph that models the historical behaviors of users and the rich content they interact with.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with only minor wording changes that do not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system has been deployed on the Web at Alipay, serving more than 100 million daily active users.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system has been deployed on the Web at Alipay, serving more than 100 million daily active users.
alignments.rationale: Deployment scale and presence on Alipay are stated identically in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Transformer-based model integrates expert rules from the knowledge graph to infer the online user’s next intent.
alignments.rationale: This publication-level technical claim about the Transformer-based model and rule integration does not appear in the preprint and is an added detail in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FedAlign outperforms various state-of-the-art (non-IID) federated classification methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedAlign demonstrates superior performance compared to various state-of-the-art non-IID federated classification methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the performance claim using equivalent language about superiority to state-of-the-art non-IID methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework aligns the latent spaces across clients from both label and data perspectives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedAlign1 effectively aligns the latent spaces across clients with non-identical class sets.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes alignment in the specific setting of non-identical class sets (a clarification/narrowing of the original, broader phrasing about label and data perspectives).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heterogeneity in client class sets poses a significant challenge for aggregating local encoders at the server.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method addresses the challenge of heterogeneous client class sets in federated learning.
alignments.rationale: Both texts state the same challenge and that the proposed method addresses it; the publication frames it as the method addressing the stated heterogeneity.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework leverages natural language class names to anchor class representations and guide data encoder learning.
alignments.rationale: This specific mechanism (using natural language class names) is present in the publication but not in the preprint, so it is an added implementation/detail claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper develops bilingual Statistical Machine Translation models for translating English to fifteen low-resource Indian Languages and vice versa.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The comparison between SMT and NMT indicates that SMT performs better in some cases while NMT outperforms in others across various languages.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claims the development of bilingual SMT models for 15 languages; the publication emphasizes comparative performance between SMT and NMT rather than explicitly restating the broad development claim, which is a revision/narrowing of emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different preprocessing approaches are proposed to handle the noise of the dataset used in the translation models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The msd-bidirectional-fe reordering performs better than the distance-based reordering model for low-resource Indic languages.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a specific preprocessing/reordering approach and result (msd-bidirectional-fe) which clarifies and narrows the preprint's more general claim about proposing preprocessing approaches.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quality of the translation is evaluated using standard metrics such as BLEU, METEOR, and RIBES.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The IL-English systems perform better than the English-IL systems for all evaluation metrics, despite being trained on the same corpus.
alignments.rationale: The publication's result refers to outcomes across the stated evaluation metrics, directly aligning with the preprint's claim about using standard metrics to evaluate translation quality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models using data with geographical attributes determine the results of the election with better precision and accuracy than conventional polling methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models using data with geographical attributes determine the results of the election with better precision and accuracy than conventional polling methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim verbatim about model performance using geographically attributed data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Analysis of public online data can outperform conventional polling methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Analysis of public online data can outperform conventional polling methods.
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion about online data outperforming polling is carried into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Political analysis and general forecasting would likely benefit from incorporating online social media data in the immediate future.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Political analysis and general forecasting would likely benefit from incorporating online social media data in the immediate future.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same forward-looking conclusion regarding benefits to political analysis and forecasting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Twitter dataset with geographical attributes is positively correlated with results from official census data on population and internet usage in Mexico.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Twitter dataset with geographical attributes is positively correlated with results from official census data on population and internet usage in Mexico.
alignments.rationale: The correlation claim with census data is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Online activity, appropriately curated, can provide an accurate representation of offline behaviour.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Online activity, appropriately curated, can provide an accurate representation of offline behaviour.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the conclusion about curated online activity representing offline behavior without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a high correlation between the speech units and phonemes and phoneme families.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a high correlation between the speech units and phonemes and phoneme families.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the result about high correlation verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The correlation between the speech units and speaker or gender is weaker compared to their correlation with phonemes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The correlation between the speech units and speaker or gender is weaker compared to their correlation with phonemes.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same comparative correlation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Redundancies were found in the extracted units, potentially due to the units' context.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Redundancies were found in the extracted units, potentially due to the units’ context.
alignments.rationale: The redundancy finding is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new, unsupervised metric to measure unit redundancies was proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new, unsupervised metric to measure unit redundancies was proposed.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of a new unsupervised metric is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New methods were developed that improve the robustness of units' clustering and show significant improvement in zero-resource speech metrics such as ABX.
alignments.pub_claim_text: New methods were developed that improve the robustness of units’ clustering and show significant improvement in zero-resource speech metrics such as ABX.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim about improved clustering robustness and ABX metric improvements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches in classifying both pain level and pain-related behaviour. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches in classifying both pain level and pain-related behaviour. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text of the claim is identical between preprint and publication, same result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Certain circumstances, such as chronic pain, could impair the objective measures and accuracy of biometric data analysis methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Certain circumstances, such as chronic pain, could impair the objective measures and accuracy of biometric data analysis methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and caveat are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study classified pain level and pain-related behaviour in the EmoPain database. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study classified pain level and pain-related behaviour in the EmoPain database. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim content is the same though the label (contribution vs result) changed; substantive claim unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture proposed in this study incorporates multiple autoencoders using a shared training framework. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This architectural claim appears in the publication but has no corresponding claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed morphology-based framework for co-registering intravascular images to CCTA images significantly outperforms other approaches for bifurcation alignment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed morphology-based framework for co-registering intravascular images to CCTA images significantly outperforms other approaches for bifurcation alignment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical performance claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework reduces the manual effort required to conduct large-scale multi-modal clinical studies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework reduces the manual effort required to conduct large-scale multi-modal clinical studies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework enables the development of machine learning-based co-registration approaches. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework enables the development of machine learning-based co-registration approaches. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same contribution claim in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Intravascular images suffer from non-rigid distortions due to irregularities in the imaging catheter path. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Intravascular images suffer from non-rigid distortions due to irregularities in the imaging catheter path. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Limitation statement is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The standard approach to exploration in adversarial imitation learning can lead to a suboptimal local maximum if the policy sufficiently matches the expert distribution without fully learning the desired task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The standard approach to exploration in adversarial imitation learning can lead to a suboptimal local maximum if the policy sufficiently matches the expert distribution without fully learning the desired task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning from Guided Play (LfGP) leverages expert demonstrations of multiple exploratory, auxiliary tasks to improve exploration in reinforcement learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Learning from Guided Play (LfGP) leverages expert demonstrations of multiple exploratory, auxiliary tasks to enhance exploration in reinforcement learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Wording changed slightly (improve -> enhance) but the contribution is substantively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that LfGP significantly outperforms both adversarial imitation learning and behavior cloning in a multitask robotic manipulation domain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LfGP significantly outperforms both adversarial imitation learning and behavior cloning in a challenging multitask robotic manipulation domain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance claim unchanged; publication emphasizes that the domain is challenging but does not alter the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LfGP is more expert sample efficient than both adversarial imitation learning and behavior cloning. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LfGP is more expert sample efficient than both adversarial imitation learning and behavior cloning. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same efficiency claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of a toy problem reveals the coupling between a local maximum and poor exploration in adversarial imitation learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance gap between LfGP and AIL is explained by the coupling between a local maximum and poor exploration. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the toy-problem observation as an explanation for the performance gap, strengthening and broadening the original analytical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed evolutionary variational autoencoder (eVAE) integrates a variational genetic algorithm into VAE with variational evolutionary operators. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This specific architectural/algorithmic integration claim does not appear among the publication's summarized claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: eVAE addresses the KL-vanishing problem for text generation with low reconstruction loss. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: eVAE addresses the KL-vanishing problem for text generation with low reconstruction loss. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The KL-vanishing mitigation claim is present and unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: eVAE generates all disentangled factors with sharp images and improves the image generation quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: eVAE generates all the disentangled factors with sharp images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim about disentangled factors and sharp images (the preprint also mentioned image quality, which is split across publication claims).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: eVAE achieves better reconstruction loss, disentanglement, and generation-inference balance than its competitors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: eVAE achieves better disentanglement, generation performance, and generation-inference balance than its competitors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication replaces 'reconstruction loss' with 'generation performance,' altering the measured metric in the comparative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SESNet outperforms state-of-the-art models for predicting the sequence-function relationship on 26 deep mutational scanning datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SESNet outperforms state-of-the-art models for predicting the sequence-function relationship on 26 deep mutational scanning datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical benchmarking result in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SESNet achieves strikingly high accuracy in prediction of the fitness of protein mutants, especially for higher order variants (> 4 mutation sites), when finetuned by using only a small number of experimental mutation data (<50). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SESNet achieves strikingly high accuracy in prediction of the fitness of protein mutants, especially for the higher order variants (> 4 mutation sites), when finetuned by using only a small number of experimental mutation data (< 50). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim about fine-tuning performance and data-efficiency is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed data augmentation strategy by leveraging data from unsupervised models to pre-train SESNet is of great practical value. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed data augmentation strategy by leveraging the data from unsupervised models to pre-train SESNet is of great practical value. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The stated contribution regarding pre-training/augmentation is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep convolutional autoencoders tend to reject telluric lines in stellar spectra. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep convolutional autoencoders tend to reject telluric lines in stellar spectra. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are essentially identical in wording and meaning about autoencoders rejecting telluric lines.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network automatically identifies the statistical independence between stellar and telluric components only when the spectra are in the barycentric frame. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network automatically identifies the statistical independence between stellar and telluric components only when the spectra are in the barycentric frame. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about the requirement of the barycentric frame is restated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for removal of telluric lines from stellar spectra operates in a fully unsupervised fashion and increases the inter-observation entropy of telluric absorption lines. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, 'Stellar Karaoke', successfully removes telluric lines from stellar spectra in a fully unsupervised fashion. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both describe unsupervised removal of tellurics, but the publication omits the specific claim about increasing inter-observation entropy, so the claim content has been revised/trimmed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach, 'Stellar Karaoke', processes each spectrum in milliseconds and requires no prior knowledge about observation parameters. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, 'Stellar Karaoke', successfully removes telluric lines from stellar spectra in a fully unsupervised fashion. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the method name and unsupervised property but does not repeat the runtime and 'no prior knowledge' performance claims, so the specific contribution details were revised/omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A significant performance drop was observed when training and testing on Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) due to low resolution. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a significant performance drop when applying the method to Sloan Digital Sky Survey data due to low resolution. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation regarding SDSS and low resolution is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ORUGA is a method that automatically optimizes the readability of any text in English. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ORUGA is a method that automatically optimizes the readability of any text in English. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The description of ORUGA as an automatic readability optimizer is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Certain quantifiable factors affect the readability of a text, such as the number of words, syllables, and presence or absence of adverbs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this specific listing of quantifiable readability factors; that detail appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method preserves both the original text's content and form through multi-objective optimization techniques. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research preserves the original text’s content and form through multi-objective optimization techniques. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preservation of content and form via multi-objective optimization is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An exhaustive study confirms that the method optimizes the degree of readability in all cases without significantly altering the text's form or meaning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An exhaustive study confirms that the method optimized the degree of readability in all cases without significantly altering the form or meaning of the texts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental conclusion about exhaustive evaluation and negligible alteration to form/meaning is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Discriminative Radial Domain Adaptation (DRDA) enhances feature transferability and discriminability simultaneously. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Discriminative Radial Domain Adaptation (DRDA) enhances feature transferability and discriminability simultaneously. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on varied tasks, including unsupervised domain adaptation and domain generalization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on varied tasks, including unsupervised domain adaptation and domain generalization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance superiority across tasks is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DRDA utilizes a shared radial structure to bridge source and target domains. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DRDA utilizes a shared radial structure to bridge source and target domains. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological description is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method encourages samples to cluster close to corresponding local anchors based on optimal-transport assignment to enhance discriminability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method encourages samples to cluster close to corresponding local anchors based on optimal-transport assignment to enhance discriminability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The technical result about clustering to local anchors via optimal transport is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning-based human parsing solutions have made remarkable achievements. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning-based human parsing solutions have made remarkable achievements. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present quantitative performance comparisons of the reviewed methods on benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present quantitative performance comparisons of the reviewed methods on benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about presenting quantitative comparisons is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We put forward a transformer-based human parsing framework, providing a high-performance baseline for follow-up research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We put forward a transformer-based human parsing framework, providing a high-performance baseline for follow-up research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution of a transformer-based baseline remains unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We point out a set of under-investigated open issues in this field and suggest new directions for future study. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We point out a set of under-investigated open issues in this field and suggest new directions for future study. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The discussion of open issues and future directions is carried through unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The complexity of approximating the correlation clustering problem is reduced from O(m×(2+α(G))+n) to O(m+n). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed index-based approach achieves an amortized runtime of O(m·α(n)) for dynamic operations and O(m+n) for clustering queries with a fixed threshold ε. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a more detailed breakdown (amortized runtime for dynamic ops and O(m+n) for queries) than the preprint's single complexity reduction claim, narrowing and clarifying the original complexity statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach produces the same output as the original algorithm while allowing for dynamic changes in the graph. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method retains the theoretical guarantees of prior algorithms and addresses their computational bottlenecks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The index-based algorithm demonstrates a 34% decrease in time on average compared to the non-index algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments validate the practical efficiency of the approach, showing a 34% decrease in clustering runtime on average compared to non-indexed methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Constructing the index requires O(m) memory and O(m×α(G)) time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overhead memory cost for the index is O(m+n). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication revises the resource-cost statement: memory overhead is reported as O(m+n) rather than O(m), broadening (increasing) the claimed memory cost; the time-cost expression is also restated in different form elsewhere.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised based Cut-and-Paste GAN performs foreground object segmentation and generates realistic composite images without manual annotations.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim restates the annotation-free segmentation and composite-image generation; the published claims focus on discriminator/generator behavior and performance, so this specific contribution statement is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method extends the ability of standard discriminators to learn both global data representations and semantic and structural information through pseudo labels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method extends the ability of standard discriminators to learn semantic and structural information through pseudo labels.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core claim about learning semantic and structural information via pseudo labels but drops the explicit mention of 'global data representations', narrowing the scope of the original wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generator is empowered to create meaningful masks by learning informative per-pixel and global image feedback from the discriminator.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generator is empowered to create meaningful masks by learning informative per-pixel and global image feedback from the discriminator.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim verbatim, preserving meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our experiments demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods on standard benchmark datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised based Cut-and-Paste GAN significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods on standard benchmark datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the experimental superiority claim with the same emphasis and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The STCA model has an overall accuracy over 85%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The STCA model has an overall accuracy over 85% based on 1400 ground truth point samples from each year.
alignments.rationale: The published claim adds sampling details (1400 ground truth points) but preserves the original accuracy claim, so it is a clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CASTC model achieved an overall accuracy of 76%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CASTC model achieved an overall accuracy of 76% based on 348 ground truth samples of planting density.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the 76% accuracy claim while providing the ground-truth sample count, clarifying the result.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Goal-guided Transformer (GoT) variant of the Vision Transformer improves the data efficiency of the learning process by focusing on goal-relevant features.
alignments.rationale: Both statements emphasize focusing representations on goal-relevant features; the publication frames this primarily as a data-efficiency improvement rather than explicitly as navigation-performance improvement, so the mapping is close but somewhat reframed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By reducing the domain influence, biases in the data can be removed and the origin of the whole slide image can be disguised, thus enhancing patient data privacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: By reducing domain influence, our method can remove biases in the data and enhance patient data privacy.
alignments.rationale: The published phrasing shortens and slightly tones down the claim (omitting explicit wording about disguising origin) but preserves the claim about bias reduction and privacy enhancement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new mathematical framework provides a deeper understanding of deep learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed mathematical framework provides a deeper understanding of deep learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication; only minor label changes (contribution->conclusion).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework characterizes the functional properties of neural networks trained to fit data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This framework characterizes the functional properties of neural networks trained to fit data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim text and scope are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework explains the effect of weight decay regularization in neural network training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework explains the effect of weight decay regularization in neural network training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework elucidates the role of sparsity in neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework elucidates the role of sparsity in neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim across versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework explains why neural networks can perform well in high-dimensional problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework explains why neural networks can perform well in high-dimensional problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same claim and emphasis in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Entoptic Field Camera serves as a tool to explore the shift in image production through AI technologies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Entoptic Field Camera serves as a tool to explore the shift in image production through AI technologies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study investigates how the material interplay of data and models in AI technologies shapes human experiences of reality. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper outlines a research trajectory concerning the materiality and design affordances of AI technologies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication shifts emphasis from an empirical 'investigation' of material interplay to outlining a research trajectory focused on materiality/affordances, clarifying and narrowing the original focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research offers implications for critical, reflective, more-than-human, and ludic design to engage with AI technologies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study offers implications for critical, reflective, more-than-human, and ludic design in engaging with AI technologies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same set of implications and design orientations are stated in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The project contributes to AI literacy discourses through the conceptualization of an RtD research space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research contributes to AI literacy discourses by conceptualizing a Research-through-Design (RtD) research space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Direct restatement of contribution with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed GNN-based learning network performs closer to the expert algorithm and captures more intruders than other baseline algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed GNN-based learning network performs closer to the expert algorithm and captures more intruders than other baseline algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact match of performance claim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GNN-based network is trained at a small scale and can be generalized to large-scale cases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GNN-based network is trained at a small scale and can be generalized to large-scale cases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same generalization claim and certainty retained in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study addresses the challenge of making perimeter defense strategies scalable for large-scale problem instances. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study addresses the challenge of making perimeter defense strategies scalable for large-scale problem instances. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution statement is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Explainable Feature Construction (EFC) methodology identifies groups of co-occurring attributes exposed by popular explanation methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Explainable Feature Construction (EFC) methodology identifies groups of co-occurring attributes exposed by popular explanation methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Methodological contribution is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reducing the search to these groups significantly reduces the time of feature construction using various constructive operators. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reducing the search to these groups significantly reduces the time of feature construction using various constructive operators. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance/time reduction claim is retained exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EFC effectively identifies informative groups of attributes and constructs relevant features as shown in an analysis on 10 transparent synthetic datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EFC effectively identifies informative groups of attributes and constructs relevant features on 10 transparent synthetic datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same experimental claim and dataset size; wording minorly adjusted but meaning preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using 30 real-world classification datasets, EFC shows significant improvements in classification accuracy for several classifiers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using 30 real-world classification datasets, EFC shows significant improvements in classification accuracy for several classifiers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Empirical improvement claim preserved identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EFC generated interpretable features on a real-world problem from the financial industry, which were confirmed by a domain expert. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EFC generated interpretable features on a real-world problem from the financial industry, which were confirmed by a domain expert. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Content is the same but the reported certainty was lowered from high to medium in the publication, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning can effectively classify different core-collapse supernovae signals and noise transients using real data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning demonstrates high potential for multi-label classification of different core-collapse supernovae simulated signals and noise transients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication shifts from claiming effective classification on real data to demonstrating high potential on simulated signals, which revises and narrows the original claim and softens wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of 1D and 2D convolutional neural networks was compared on single and multiple detector data for CCSNe signal classification. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of 1D and 2D convolutional neural networks on single detector data shows overall accuracies of approximately 99% for LIGO and 80% for Virgo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses results on single-detector performance (narrowing the original broader comparison) and reports stronger certainty/precision of accuracies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall classification accuracy for LIGO was approximately 99%, while for Virgo it was around 80%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of 1D and 2D convolutional neural networks on single detector data shows overall accuracies of approximately 99% for LIGO and 80% for Virgo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Metric values and context for single-detector accuracies are preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the multi-detector case, an accuracy of approximately 98% was achieved using triggers coincident among the three interferometer detectors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of multi-detector data using triggers coincident among the three interferometers achieves an accuracy of approximately 98%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Multi-detector result is stated consistently between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method generates realistic anatomical deformations from static patient scans using deep learning driven respiratory motion simulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structure similarity index measure (SSIM) for predicted breathing phases and ground truth 4D CT images was 0.92 ± 0.04, demonstrating RMSim's potential to generate realistic respiratory motion.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides quantitative SSIM evidence supporting the preprint's claim of generating realistic respiratory motion, which clarifies and strengthens the original claim without changing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 3D Seq2Seq deep learning respiratory motion simulator (RMSim) predicts future breathing phases from static CT images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structure similarity index measure (SSIM) for predicted breathing phases and ground truth 4D CT images was 0.92 ± 0.04, demonstrating RMSim's potential to generate realistic respiratory motion.
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies similarity between predicted phases and ground truth, which clarifies and supports the claim that RMSim predicts future breathing phases.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RMSim was validated using both private and public benchmark datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness in generating deformation augmentations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The landmark registration error in a public DIR dataset was improved from 8.12 ± 5.78 mm to 6.58mm ± 6.38 mm using RMSim-augmented training data.
alignments.rationale: The publication supplies a concrete public-dataset validation demonstrating effectiveness (amplifying confidence) but does not explicitly report the private validation mentioned in the preprint, narrowing the claimed scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach can be utilized for validating deformable image registration (DIR) algorithms and for patient-specific augmentations to enhance deep learning DIR algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach can be used for validating DIR algorithms as well as for patient-specific augmentations to improve deep learning DIR algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about use for DIR validation and patient-specific augmentations with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised generative representation effectively learns gait-motion-related patterns for Parkinson's Disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model effectively detects gait parkinsonian patterns recorded in markerless videos, even achieving competitive results with classes not observed during training.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the learned representation as effective detection of parkinsonian gait patterns and adds details about performance on unseen classes, clarifying the original representation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architecture achieves an AUC of 95% in the classification task when validated with 14 PD patients and 23 control subjects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised generative representation achieves a ROC-AUC of 95% in the classification task using the owner dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same 95% ROC-AUC on the authors' (owner) dataset, matching the preprint's performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach maintains a homocedasticity level of 70% and a shapeness level of 70% in its classification task.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or reiterate the specific homocedasticity or shapeness metrics claimed in the preprint, indicating these claims were omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The self-supervised learning method avoids inter-class variance, allowing for better representation of locomotion relationships.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model effectively detects gait parkinsonian patterns recorded in markerless videos, even achieving competitive results with classes not observed during training.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes practical generalization and detection, including unseen classes, which clarifies and broadens the preprint's conceptual claim about avoidance of inter-class variance and strengthens empirical confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed universal domain adaptation setting for remote sensing image scene classification requires no prior knowledge on the label sets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed universal domain adaptation (UniDA) setting for remote sensing image scene classification requires no prior knowledge on the label sets.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same UniDA setting claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel universal domain adaptation method without source data is effective for cases when the source data is unavailable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel UniDA method without source data is effective for cases when the source data are unavailable.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same method and claim of effectiveness when source data are unavailable.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model architecture includes a source data generation stage and a model adaptation stage to facilitate classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model architecture includes a source data generation stage and a model adaptation stage to facilitate classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the architectural components described in the preprint without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model successfully recognizes 'unknown' samples while classifying target samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model successfully recognizes 'unknown' samples while promoting adaptation in the shared label set.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the nuance that recognizing unknowns occurs alongside promoting adaptation in the shared label set, which clarifies but does not change the original result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The debris-covered glacial ice segmentation model trained using self-learning boundary-aware loss outperformed the model trained using dice loss.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The debris-covered glacial ice segmentation model trained using self-learning boundary-aware loss outperformed the model trained using dice loss.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative performance claim directly from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Red, shortwave infrared, and near-infrared bands have the highest contribution toward debris-covered glacial ice segmentation from Landsat 7 images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Red, shortwave infrared, and near-infrared bands have the highest contribution toward debris-covered glacial ice segmentation from Landsat 7 images.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about band contributions without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A modified version of the U-Net is proposed for large-scale, spatially non-overlapping, clean glacial ice, and debris-covered glacial ice segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A modified version of the U-Net is proposed for large-scale, spatially non-overlapping, clean glacial ice, and debris-covered glacial ice segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same model contribution as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel self-learning boundary-aware loss is introduced to improve debris-covered glacial ice segmentation performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel self-learning boundary-aware loss is introduced to improve debris-covered glacial ice segmentation performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same methodological contribution and claim from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed action mapping approach improves the learning process by dividing it into two steps: learning feasibility and then the objective.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed action mapping approach improves the learning process by dividing it into two steps: learning feasibility and then the objective.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conceptual contribution without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feasibility part of the approach generates all feasible actions through self-supervised querying of the feasibility model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feasibility part of the approach generates all feasible actions through self-supervised querying of the feasibility model.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the feasibility-generation mechanism as described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The agent demonstrates proficiency in generating actions across disconnected feasible action sets in robotic path planning and grasping scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The agent demonstrates proficiency in generating actions across disconnected feasible action sets in robotic path planning and grasping scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same empirical proficiency claim as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By addressing the feasibility step, the paper paves the way for a reinforcement learning framework that is both safe and efficient.
alignments.pub_claim_text: By addressing the feasibility step, the paper paves the way for a reinforcement learning framework that is both safe and efficient.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same concluding statement regarding safety and efficiency in RL frameworks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We introduce a novel and computationally efficient method for vertex embedding, community detection, and community size determination.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce a novel and computationally efficient method for vertex embedding, community detection, and community size determination.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact claim with no substantive change in wording, scope, or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach leverages a normalized one-hot graph encoder and a rank-based cluster size measure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach leverages a normalized one-hot graph encoder and a rank-based cluster size measure.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim to the publication with no change in meaning or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the excellent numerical performance of our proposed graph encoder ensemble algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the excellent numerical performance of our proposed graph encoder ensemble algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is identical in the publication, indicating no semantic, scope, or confidence change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants who overestimate their performance tend to exhibit under-reliance on AI systems, which hinders optimal team performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants who overestimate their performance tend to exhibit under-reliance on AI systems, which hinders optimal team performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this empirical finding with the same wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tutorial intervention was highly effective in helping users calibrate their self-assessment and facilitating appropriate reliance among participants with overestimated self-assessment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tutorial intervention was highly effective in helping users calibrate their self-assessment and facilitating appropriate reliance among participants with overestimated self-assessment.
alignments.rationale: The claim about tutorial effectiveness is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tutorial intervention can potentially hurt the appropriate reliance of participants with underestimated self-assessment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tutorial intervention can potentially hurt the appropriate reliance of participants with underestimated self-assessment.
alignments.rationale: Text is unchanged, but the publication labels this as a limitation rather than a primary result, indicating a tempered framing of confidence/importance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Logic units-based explanations did not help users in either improving the calibration of their competence or facilitating appropriate reliance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Logic units-based explanations did not help users in either improving the calibration of their competence or facilitating appropriate reliance.
alignments.rationale: The null effect of logic units-based explanations is reported identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings advance the current understanding of the role of self-assessment in shaping trust and reliance in human-AI decision making.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings advance the current understanding of the role of self-assessment in shaping trust and reliance in human-AI decision making.
alignments.rationale: The concluding interpretation is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SynthMorph demonstrates high accuracy in joint affine-deformable registration of brain images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SynthMorph demonstrates high accuracy in joint affine-deformable registration of brain images.
alignments.rationale: The core result about high accuracy is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SynthMorph leverages a strategy that trains networks with widely varying images synthesized from label maps, yielding robust performance for image types unseen at training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of widely varying images synthesized from label maps during training yields robust performance across acquisition specifics unseen at training.
