alignments.preprint_claim_text: SynthMorph optimizes the spatial overlap of select anatomical labels, enabling networks to distinguish anatomy of interest from irrelevant structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SynthMorph optimizes the spatial overlap of select anatomical labels, enabling networks to distinguish anatomy of interest from irrelevant structures.
alignments.rationale: This methodological claim appears verbatim in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SynthMorph combines the affine model with a deformable hypernetwork, allowing users to choose the optimal deformation-field regularity for their specific data.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about combining an affine model with a deformable hypernetwork and user-selectable regularity does not appear among the publication claims and thus appears removed from the highlighted publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks (CNN) can be effectively used to fit nuisance models in semiparametric estimation of the average causal effect of a treatment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks (CNN) can be effectively used to fit nuisance models in semiparametric estimation of the average causal effect of a treatment.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method using CNN yields efficient and uniformly valid inference when estimating the effect of early retirement on health outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method using CNN yields efficient and uniformly valid inference when estimating the effect of early retirement on health outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The performance/inference claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rates of convergence for CNN equipped with the rectified linear unit activation function are provided and compared to existing results for feedforward neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rates of convergence for CNN equipped with the rectified linear unit activation function are provided and compared to existing results for feedforward neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The theoretical rates claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed estimator is evaluated and compared with other strategies through a Monte Carlo study.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed estimator is evaluated and compared with other strategies through a Monte Carlo study.
alignments.rationale: The Monte Carlo evaluation claim is present and unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Results from a study on the effect of early retirement on hospitalization are presented using data covering the whole Swedish population.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Results on the effect of early retirement on hospitalization are presented using data covering the whole Swedish population.
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly rephrases the wording (drops "from a study") but conveys the same empirical result covering the whole Swedish population; this is a minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multi-task multi-stage transitional (MMT) training framework improves the performance of neural chat translation (NCT) models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multi-task multi-stage transitional (MMT) training framework improves the performance of neural chat translation models.
alignments.rationale: The main result is preserved with only a trivial wording change (abbreviation removal) and no change in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of auxiliary tasks, such as utterance discrimination and speaker discrimination, enhances the modelling of dialogue coherence and speaker characteristics in NCT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of auxiliary tasks, such as utterance discrimination and speaker discrimination, enhances the modelling of dialogue coherence and speaker characteristics in the NCT model.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is carried over verbatim aside from a minor addition of "the NCT model"; meaning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The intermediate training phase using additional monolingual dialogues alleviates the training discrepancy between the pre-training and fine-tuning stages.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The intermediate training phase using additional monolingual dialogues alleviates the training discrepancy between the pre-training and fine-tuning stages.
alignments.rationale: The intermediate-phase claim is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gradual transition strategy from monolingual to bilingual dialogues contributes to a smoother training process for the NCT model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gradual transition strategy from monolingual to bilingual dialogues contributes to a smoother training process for the NCT model.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this training-strategy claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments on two language pairs demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed training framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on two language pairs demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed training framework.
alignments.rationale: The experimental validation claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed transformer-based multimodal network (TranSOP) effectively predicts the functional outcome of stroke treatment based on the modified Rankin Scale (mRS). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed transformer-based multimodal network (TranSOP) effectively predicts the functional outcome of stroke treatment based on the modified Rankin Scale (mRS). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are word-for-word the same claim about TranSOP's effectiveness on mRS.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TranSOP model employs a fusion module to efficiently combine 3D non-contrast computed tomography (NCCT) features and clinical information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TranSOP model employs a fusion module to efficiently combine 3D non-contrast computed tomography (NCCT) features and clinical information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical description of the model's fusion module appears in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In comparative experiments, the TranSOP model achieves a state-of-the-art AUC score of 0.85 on the MRCLEAN dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In comparative experiments, the TranSOP model achieves a state of the art AUC score of 0.85 on the MRCLEAN dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported experimental result (AUC 0.85 on MRCLEAN) is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current mainstream AI lacks the capability of reflection, which is a core feature that humans bring to tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current mainstream AI systems lack the capability of reflection, which is a core feature that humans bring to tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about lack of reflection is effectively identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The absence of reflection in AI systems results in an unusual imbalance of insight and understanding compared to human minds. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The absence of reflection in AI affects its ability to deal with ambiguity, emergent knowledge, and social context. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces a broad claim about an "imbalance of insight and understanding" with specific affected abilities (ambiguity, emergent knowledge, social context), changing and narrowing the nature of the asserted effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper proposes an architecture for reflective AI agents based on concepts from complex systems and cognitive science. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper proposes an architecture for reflective AI agents based on concepts from complex systems and cognitive science. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing the proposed architecture is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is crucial to identify which features of human minds have been replicated in AI and which are missing. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This specific meta-level limitation/requirement appears in the preprint but is not present in the published claims list, so it was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current experiments at the LHC do not indicate clear signs of new physics that could guide the development of additional BSM theories. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current experiments do not indicate clear signs of new physics that could guide the development of additional Beyond Standard Model (BSM) theories. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement about lack of clear signs of new physics is semantically identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a novel strategy for anomaly detection in a supervised learning setting based on the artificial creation of anomalies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel strategy to perform anomaly detection in a supervised learning setting, based on the artificial creation of anomalies through a random process. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that the artificial anomalies are created through a random process and raises certainty, so the claim is clarified and its confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We successfully apply classical and quantum Support Vector Classifiers to identify artificial anomalies among the SM events. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We successfully apply classical and quantum support vector classifiers (CSVC and QSVC respectively) to identify the artificial anomalies among the SM events. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same result claim with minor stylistic expansion (abbreviations added) but no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Employing an SVC trained to identify artificial anomalies allows for the identification of realistic BSM events with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Employing an SVC trained to identify the artificial anomalies allows for the identification of realistic BSM events with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about SVC identifying realistic BSM events is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We explore the potential of quantum algorithms for improving classification accuracy and provide conditions for their optimal use. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We explore the potential of quantum algorithms for improving the classification accuracy and provide plausible conditions for the best exploitation of this novel computational paradigm. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases to emphasize "plausible conditions" and "best exploitation," clarifying the original exploration; certainty remains similar so confidence is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed strategy for anomaly detection tasks at the LHC based on unsupervised quantum machine learning demonstrates effectiveness in identifying new phenomena. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed strategy for anomaly detection tasks at the LHC based on unsupervised quantum machine learning is effective in identifying new phenomena. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim of effectiveness is equivalent in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed quantum models significantly outperform classical counterparts in kernel-based anomaly detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed quantum models significantly outperform classical counterparts in kernel-based anomaly detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about quantum models outperforming classical counterparts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The observed performance enhancement of the quantum models is related to the quantum resources utilized by the model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The observed performance enhancement of the quantum models is related to the quantum resources utilized by the model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion linking performance enhancement to quantum resources is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The aircraft maintenance, repair and overhaul industry is transitioning to 3D scanning for dent inspection, which allows for quick and repeatable measurements. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The aircraft MRO industry is transitioning to 3D scanning for dent inspection, which allows for quick and repeatable measurements. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same factual claim about the industry's transition to 3D scanning appears in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The traditional approach to dent evaluation, which only considers length, width, and depth, leads to ambiguity in classifying different dent shapes. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The traditional approach to dent evaluation in the SRM leads to ambiguity by classifying different shapes as the same. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation about the traditional metric-based approach causing shape-ambiguity is equivalent in both texts despite wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A 7-parameter model is proposed to describe the actual dent shape, enabling better utilization of high-fidelity data from 3D scanners. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A seven-parameter model is proposed to describe the actual dent shape, enabling better use of high-fidelity data from 3D scanners. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same contribution claim; only minor orthographic differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach has been evaluated in simulations and point cloud data, suggesting increased capability to evaluate damage and enabling more targeted interventions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach has been evaluated in simulations and point cloud data, suggesting an increased capability in damage evaluation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the evaluation and damage-evaluation claim but omits the explicit phrasing that it enables more targeted interventions, narrowing the claim's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of the proposed model could ultimately lead to cost savings in the aircraft maintenance process. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new model enables more targeted interventions and can ultimately save costs in the MRO industry. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the pathway to cost savings explicit (enabling targeted interventions) while preserving the original conclusion about potential cost savings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Performance unfairness among variables exists in multivariate time series forecasting models.
alignments.rationale: The publication claims focus on the proposed method and its effectiveness but does not explicitly state the background observation that performance unfairness among variables exists, so the preprint assertion was dropped in the published text.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework, FairFor, effectively addresses the fairness problem in multivariate time series forecasting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on four public datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of FairFor for fair forecasting and significant performance improvement.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents experimental evidence of effectiveness and significant improvement, which clarifies and supports the preprint's claim that FairFor addresses fairness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FairFor generates both group-independent and group-relevant representations for downstream forecasting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FairFor uses adversarial learning to generate both group-irrelevant and group-relevant representations for downstream forecasting.
alignments.rationale: The published claim retains the core statement about generating group-(ir)relevant representations but adds the mechanism (adversarial learning), so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate significant performance improvement of FairFor on four public datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on four public datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of FairFor for fair forecasting and significant performance improvement.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state that experiments on four datasets show significant performance improvement; the publication additionally emphasizes fairness, but the core experimental result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning has significantly advanced the field of industrial Image Anomaly Detection (IAD).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning has significantly advanced industrial image anomaly detection techniques.
alignments.rationale: The published statement mirrors the preprint's high-level conclusion about the impact of deep learning on industrial IAD.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper provides a comprehensive review of various deep learning-based image anomaly detection techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper reviews deep learning-based image anomaly detection techniques from various perspectives including neural network architectures and levels of supervision.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the review claim by specifying perspectives covered (architectures, supervision), thereby clarifying the nature of the comprehensive review.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The authors propose a new setting for industrial manufacturing and review current IAD approaches within this context.
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not state that a new industrial-manufacturing-specific setting was proposed; that specific contribution appears omitted in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several challenges for image anomaly detection are highlighted in the paper.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several challenges for image anomaly detection are highlighted in the paper.
alignments.rationale: The challenges highlighted in the preprint are retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing neural operators do not necessarily perform well for all physics problems, particularly high-frequency waves.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing neural operators do not necessarily perform well for all physics problems, particularly high-frequency waves.
alignments.rationale: The limitation about existing neural operators and high-frequency waves is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed subfamily of neural operators enables an enhanced empirical approximation of the nonlinear operator mapping wave speed to solution for the Helmholtz equation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed subfamily of neural operators enables an enhanced empirical approximation of the nonlinear operator mapping wave speed to solution for the Helmholtz equation.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication present the same contribution regarding the subfamily enabling enhanced empirical approximation for the Helmholtz map.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experiments reveal that the introduction of stochastic depth improves generalization and performance of the proposed neural operators compared to standard neural operators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our neural operators demonstrate superior performance compared to standard neural operators in both training and out-of-distribution scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the experimental finding more strongly and explicitly across training and OOD scenarios, amplifying confidence relative to the preprint's medium-certainty result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An upper bound tied to the stochastic depth of the modified models is proven, which existing neural operators do not satisfy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We prove an upper bound tied to the stochastic depth of our modified models that existing neural operators do not satisfy.
alignments.rationale: The proven upper bound and its distinction from existing operators is presented equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel out-of-distribution risk bound tailored to Gaussian measures on Banach spaces is obtained, relating stochastic depth with the bound.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We obtain a novel out-of-distribution risk bound tailored to Gaussian measures on Banach spaces, relating stochastic depth with the bound.
alignments.rationale: The technical bound is present in both; the publication lists it with slightly lower certainty (medium), so the confidence is modestly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study investigates a semidefinite programming (SDP) approach to the community recovery problem on a multilayer hypergraph stochastic block model (HSBM).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study investigates a semidefinite programming (SDP) approach to the community recovery problem on a multilayer hypergraph stochastic block model (HSBM).
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing the SDP approach to multilayer HSBM is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Information-theoretic conditions are obtained that guarantee exact recovery of communities in both assortative and disassortative cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Information-theoretic conditions on the model parameters are established that guarantee exact recovery in both assortative and disassortative cases.
alignments.rationale: The published claim is the same substantive result but adds explicit mention that the conditions are on model parameters, clarifying the statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model parameters play a crucial role in determining the conditions for exact recovery in the community recovery problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Information-theoretic conditions on the model parameters are established that guarantee exact recovery in both assortative and disassortative cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly ties the recovery conditions to model parameters, which aligns with and clarifies the preprint's conclusion about the importance of parameters.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large language models (LLMs) such as GPT-4 have demonstrated impressive results across a wide range of tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large language models (LLMs) such as GPT-4 have demonstrated impressive results across a wide range of tasks.
alignments.rationale: The statement about LLMs' impressive results is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs frequently fail at complex reasoning and their reasoning processes are opaque.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs frequently fail at complex reasoning and their reasoning processes are opaque.
alignments.rationale: The limitation regarding failures on complex reasoning and opacity is retained identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ThoughtSource is a meta-dataset and software library designed to improve qualitative understanding of chain-of-thought reasoning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ThoughtSource is a meta-dataset and software library designed to improve qualitative understanding of chain-of-thought reasoning.
alignments.rationale: The description of ThoughtSource as a meta-dataset and library is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first release of ThoughtSource integrates seven scientific/medical, three general-domain, and five math word question answering datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The first release of ThoughtSource integrates seven scientific/medical, three general-domain, and five math word question answering datasets.
alignments.rationale: The dataset composition in the first release is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed time-sensitive federated learning (TS-FL) framework minimizes the overall run-time for collaboratively training a shared ML model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed time-sensitive federated learning (TS-FL) framework minimizes the overall run-time for training a shared ML model while maintaining desirable accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core contribution but adds the requirement of maintaining accuracy, which clarifies the objective rather than changing it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training acceleration solutions for TS-FL with synchronous coordination (TS-FL-SC) and asynchronous coordination (TS-FL-ASC) are investigated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TS-FL-SC and TS-FL-ASC can provide up to 63% and 28% reduction in the overall model training time, respectively, compared with traditional solutions.
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from stating investigation of solutions to reporting quantified benefits for those two approaches, clarifying the outcome of the investigation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results show that TS-FL-SC can provide up to 63% reduction in the overall model training time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TS-FL-SC and TS-FL-ASC can provide up to 63% and 28% reduction in the overall model training time, respectively, compared with traditional solutions.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative result for TS-FL-SC (63% reduction) within a combined statement; the claim content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results show that TS-FL-ASC can provide up to 28% reduction in the overall model training time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TS-FL-SC and TS-FL-ASC can provide up to 63% and 28% reduction in the overall model training time, respectively, compared with traditional solutions.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same quantitative result for TS-FL-ASC (28% reduction); the content is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The boundaries between human and machine are not predetermined but are enacted within a certain material configuration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The boundaries between human and machine are not predetermined but are enacted within a certain material configuration.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conceptual conclusion verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integrated AI system, Message Ritual, encourages the re-framing of memory through machine generated poetics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Message Ritual is a novel, integrated AI system that encourages the re-framing of memory through machine generated poetics.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds descriptors (e.g., 'novel') but retains the same claim about the system's function, clarifying its positioning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lamp listens to conversations and reconstitutes key topics and phrases into poetry delivered via SMS.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system listens in on conversations and reconstitutes key topics and phrases into machine generated poetry delivered via SMS.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same operational behavior of the system with minor wording changes (lamp → system, machine generated added) that do not alter the claim's meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants across four households lived with the lamp for a two week period, allowing for a diffractive analysis of its interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants across four households lived with the lamp for a two week period, allowing for a diffractive analysis of its interaction with them.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same study and duration; wording adjustments (interaction with them) do not change the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method of using the lamp has implications for future HCI research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implications of the method used in this study are discussed for future HCI research.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames this as a discussed implication rather than a general statement, clarifying that implications are explicitly addressed in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Physics-inspired model designs enable successful reconstruction of vortical flows from spatially limited measurements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Physics-inspired model designs enable successful reconstruction of vortical flows from spatially limited measurements.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this technical result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper surveys a variety of recent super-resolution applications in the context of machine-learning-based reconstruction for vortical flows.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper surveys a variety of recent super-resolution applications in the context of vortical flows.
alignments.rationale: The survey claim is retained with equivalent scope and emphasis in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Insights gained from this study can be leveraged for super-resolution analysis of numerical and experimental flow data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Insights gained from this study can be leveraged for super-resolution analysis of numerical and experimental flow data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this concluding statement unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The UESTC-MMEA-CL dataset facilitates research in continual learning for egocentric activity recognition by providing synchronized data from videos, accelerometers, and gyroscopes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The UESTC-MMEA-CL dataset is proposed to address the shortage of multi-modal egocentric activity datasets for continual activity learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as addressing a dataset shortage (more explicit aim) while preserving the dataset's role in facilitating continual learning research.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset includes data for 32 types of daily activities performed by 10 participants, which is compared with other publicly available datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset contains synchronized data of video, accelerometers, and gyroscopes for 32 types of daily activities performed by 10 participants.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same dataset composition but with stronger certainty; the comparative remark from the preprint is omitted, increasing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The statistical analysis of the sensor data demonstrates auxiliary effects for different behaviors in egocentric activity recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Statistical analysis of the sensor data demonstrates the auxiliary effects of activity recognition.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same empirical finding with higher certainty, strengthening the claim's confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The problem of catastrophic forgetting in continual learning for egocentric activity recognition remains unexplored due to the lack of available datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of four baseline methods on UESTC-MMEA-CL explores the catastrophic forgetting problem in continual learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the claim from a stated gap ('remains unexplored') to reporting that the authors explore catastrophic forgetting via evaluations, revising and narrowing the original limitation while increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Coordinated communities feature variable degrees of temporal instability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Coordinated communities (CCs) feature variable degrees of temporal instability.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this empirical result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamic analyses are needed to account for the instability of coordinated communities, as results of static analyses can be unreliable and scarcely representative.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dynamic analyses are needed to account for the instability of coordinated communities, as results of static analyses can be unreliable and scarcely representative.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological conclusion without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some users exhibit distinct archetypal behaviors that have important practical implications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some users exhibit distinct archetypal behaviors that have important practical implications.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this finding with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Content and network characteristics contribute to explaining why users leave and join coordinated communities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Content and network characteristics contribute to explaining why users leave and join coordinated communities.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this explanatory result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results demonstrate the advantages of dynamic analyses and open up new directions of research on online debates and coordinated communities.
alignments.rationale: This broader claim about opening new research directions is not present in the publication's listed claims and appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The problem of calculating the probability of an individual solution being selected under lexicase selection, named lex-prob, is NP-Hard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The problem of calculating selection probabilities under lexicase selection, named lex-prob, is proven to be NP-Hard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same formal result that lex-prob is NP-Hard with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proof of lex-prob's NP-Hardness is achieved by reducing SAT, an NP-Complete problem, to lex-prob in polynomial time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proof of lex-prob's NP-Hardness is achieved by reducing SAT, a well-known NP-Hard problem, to lex-prob in polynomial time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe the same reduction from SAT to lex-prob; the publication’s wording about SAT does not change the substance of the proof claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Finding a fast way to calculate the probabilities of individual solutions being selected under lexicase selection in polynomial time is incredibly challenging, if not altogether impossible. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A highly-optimized brute-force algorithm for calculating selection probabilities is presented, capable of solving realistically-sized instances in approximately 15 seconds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed practical impossibility; the publication presents a practical (though brute-force) algorithm that solves realistic instances, changing the practical conclusion to a more limited/achievable outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The result has important practical implications for developing approximation algorithms or practical optimizations for lexicase selection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A highly-optimized brute-force algorithm for calculating selection probabilities is presented, capable of solving realistically-sized instances in approximately 15 seconds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication concretely demonstrates practical implications by providing an optimized algorithm, which clarifies and strengthens the preprint’s suggestion about practical relevance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proof has deeper theoretical implications about the relationship between epsilon-lexicase selection and lexicase selection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reduction from ∊-lexicase selection to standard lexicase selection has deeper theoretical implications about their relationship. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state the same theoretical implication about the relationship between ε-lexicase and standard lexicase selection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepONets can accurately learn the solution operators, achieving prediction accuracy scores above 0.96 for the ODE and diffusion problems over the observed domain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep Operator Networks (DeepONets) can accurately learn the solution operators, achieving prediction accuracy (R²) scores above 0.96 for the ODE and diffusion problems over the observed domain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative accuracy result (R²>0.96) for the same problems, preserving the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained models exhibit excellent generalization ability when evaluated on unseen scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained models exhibit excellent generalization ability when evaluated on unseen scenarios, demonstrating zero-shot capability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the specific characterization 'zero-shot', which clarifies and strengthens the original generalization claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ONets represent a robust and generalizable alternative for approximating partial differential equations solutions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ONets represent a robust and generalizable alternative for approximating partial differential equations (PDEs) solutions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same contribution but with higher stated certainty, effectively amplifying confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results confirm the promise of ONets and motivate further enhancements to the DeepONet algorithm. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: While convection–diffusion poses a greater challenge, the results confirm the promise of ONets and motivate further enhancements to the DeepONet algorithm. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the motivating conclusion but adds a limitation (convection–diffusion difficulty), which clarifies and slightly tempers the original optimistic statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model successfully learns to perform multi-digit additions and subtractions, achieving an error rate below 1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model successfully learns to perform multi-digit additions and subtractions, achieving an error rate below 1%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative performance result with the same wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrates the ability to solve arithmetic problems even when operands are much longer than those observed during training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model demonstrates the ability to solve arithmetic problems by interacting with a virtual abacus. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a specific mechanism (virtual abacus interaction) rather than explicit extrapolation to longer operands; this changes the interpretation of how the model achieves the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of learning agents varies based on the amount of explicit supervision they receive. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of learning agents varies based on the amount of explicit supervision they receive. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same relationship between supervision level and performance, with only a minor shift in labeling (conclusion vs result).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of error patterns helps to understand the limitations and biases resulting from the design choices made in the model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of error patterns helps to understand the limitations and biases resulting from the design choices. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same diagnostic conclusion about error analysis and model limitations with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end encoder-decoder model with a layer-wise selection mechanism improves translation quality by refining the long document context. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The layer-wise selection mechanism helps to sift and refine the long document context, improving translation quality. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims attribute improved translation quality to the layer-wise selection mechanism refining long-context information; the publication frames it as a conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments show that the model significantly outperforms previous models on all datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end encoder-decoder model with a layer-wise selection mechanism significantly outperforms previous models on all datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same empirical claim of significant outperformance across datasets with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The soft selection mechanism effectively mitigates the noise introduced by irrelevant or trivial words in the context. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Irrelevant or trivial words in the context can introduce noise and distract the model from learning the relationship between the current sentence and auxiliary context. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims identify context noise from irrelevant words and imply the selection mechanism mitigates that problem; the publication emphasizes the noise effect explicitly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm achieves an average Dice similarity coefficient of $0.628\pm0.033$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm achieves an average Dice of 62.8 ± 3.3 %. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric values are the same (converted between fraction and percent) and therefore the result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sensitivity of the proposed model is $0.699\pm0.039$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The segmentation method shows a sensitivity of 69.9 ± 3.9 % and specificity of 99.7 ± 0.2 %. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Sensitivity values match numerically (0.699 = 69.9%), so the claim is unchanged and directly supported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The specificity of the proposed model is $0.9965\pm0.0016$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The segmentation method shows a sensitivity of 69.9 ± 3.9 % and specificity of 99.7 ± 0.2 %. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specificity reported in the publication (99.7%) corresponds to the preprint's value (0.9965), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The precision of the proposed model is $0.619\pm0.036$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Precision is reported in the preprint but does not appear among the publication claims, indicating it was omitted or removed in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model shows promising segmentation results for acute ischemic stroke lesions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm provides a Dice improvement of 12.0 % over the baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a concrete quantitative improvement (12% Dice gain) that substantiates and sharpens the preprint’s general claim of promising segmentation results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper presents an end-to-end pipeline to automate the generation of 3D virtual tours using equirectangular images for real-estate properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents an end-to-end pipeline to automate the generation of 3D virtual tours using equirectangular images for real-estate properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same end-to-end pipeline contribution with matching scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed HSV-based coloring scheme for paper tags improves the learning of tag detection and digit recognition tasks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed HSV-based coloring scheme for paper tags improves the learning of tag detection and digit recognition tasks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim is effectively identical in content and confidence to the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficiency of the proposed pipeline is demonstrated on a real-world equirectangular image dataset collected from the Housing.com database. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficiency of the proposed pipeline is demonstrated on a real-world equirectangular image dataset collected from the Housing.com database. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The demonstration on the same Housing.com dataset is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Case-Base Neural Networks (CBNNs) outperform regression and neural network-based survival methods in a simulation involving a complex baseline hazard and time-varying interactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Case-Base Neural Networks (CBNNs) outperform regression and neural network-based survival methods in a simulation involving a complex baseline hazard and time-varying interactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The simulation result is restated exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CBNNs outperform competing models in two real data applications and show similar performance in a third application. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CBNNs outperform competing models in two real data applications and show similar performance in a third application. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication mirrors the preprint claim about real-data application performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of case-base sampling with deep learning provides a simple and flexible framework for data-driven modeling of single event survival outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of case-base sampling with deep learning provides a simple and flexible framework for data-driven modeling of single event survival outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The methodological conclusion is preserved unchanged in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explainable AI (XAI) and interpretable machine learning (IML) are crucial for the accurate prediction of remaining useful life (RUL) in digital twin systems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of explainable AI (XAI) and interpretable machine learning (IML) is crucial for the accurate prediction of prognostics, such as remaining useful life (RUL), in a digital twin system. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim about the crucial role of XAI/IML for RUL prediction with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using explainable and interpretable AI can lead to more accurate predictions of RUL, resulting in better maintenance and repair planning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Intelligent digital twin systems can make more accurate predictions of RUL by using an explainable, interpretable, and trustworthy AI. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the link between XAI and improved RUL prediction but drops the downstream claim about maintenance/repair planning, narrowing the scope of the outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of XAI and IML is necessary to ensure the trustworthiness of AI/ML applications for RUL prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper justifies the important role of AI/ML for the digital twin components, which requires XAI to understand the prediction better. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the integration as a requirement to understand predictions and justifies the role of XAI; wording shifts slightly but the core claim about XAI ensuring trustworthy predictions is retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI models must be fair, unbiased, and explainable to gain confidence in their trustworthiness. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Intelligent digital twin systems can make more accurate predictions of RUL by using an explainable, interpretable, and trustworthy AI. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes explainability and trustworthiness but omits explicit claims about fairness/unbiasedness; the absolute normative 'must' is softened to emphasis on explainability, indicating a tempered claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: YeLan outperforms the baseline models in challenging conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: YeLan surpasses baseline models under strenuous conditions and exhibits resilience against varying clothing types, background motion, viewing angles, occlusions, and lighting fluctuations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands on the types of challenging conditions where YeLan outperforms baselines, adding detail and broadening the scope of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The event camera is a promising solution to overcome the shortcomings of existing RGB and RGB-Depth camera solutions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current RGB and RGB-Depth camera solutions for dance gaming applications are hindered by low-light conditions and excessive power consumption. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the limitation of RGB-based solutions (implying why event cameras are promising). The original positive statement about event cameras is reframed as documenting limitations of alternatives, a clarifying shift in focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: YeLan survives low-lighting conditions and dynamic backgrounds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: YeLan, a neuromorphic camera-driven, three-dimensional, high-frequency human pose estimation system, is capable of withstanding low-light environments and dynamic backgrounds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability (withstanding low-light and dynamic backgrounds) with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We collected the world's first event camera dance dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The first-ever neuromorphic camera dance human pose estimation dataset has been compiled. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same contribution claim about assembling the first neuromorphic/event camera dance dataset.

alignments.pub_claim_text: TDSTF achieved a Standardized Average Continuous Ranked Probability Score (SACRPS) of 0.4438 and a Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 0.4168, representing improvements of 18.9% and 34.3% over the best baseline model, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: TDSTF is an effective and efficient solution for forecasting vital signs in the ICU. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
